automatic.title: 自動ソーラーパネル洗浄ロボット導入事例
automatic.description: 自動ソーラーパネル洗浄ロボットの導入事例。AI自律清掃で高効率とROIを実現。
automatic.introP1Before: This portfolio groups Indian utility-scale plants where Taypro deploys fully
automatic.introP1Link: automatic GLYDE and GLYDE-X cleaning robots
automatic.introP1After: on fixed-tilt and single-axis tracker arrays. Fleets run on waterless dual-pass schedules, coordinated through NECTYR monitoring, so O&M teams recover generation lost to soiling without manual washing crews.
automatic.introP2: Automatic projects typically serve 50 MW+ sites with predictable row geometry, high dust load, or water scarcity. Asset owners choose autonomous robots when cycle frequency, labour safety, and performance ratio stability matter more than occasional portable coverage. Each case study below documents capacity, location, and how robots were commissioned on site.
automatic.introP3Middle: to model payback for your plant, or return to the full
automatic.introP3After: to compare semi-automatic and CAPEX listings.
automatic.highlightsHeading: What automatic Taypro projects have in common

highlights.h0: Autonomous dry cleaning with AI-driven cycle planning and weather-aware hold windows.
highlights.h1: Fleet visibility in NECTYR, block status, robot health, and cleaning history for O&M leads.
highlights.h2: Documented commissioning on multi-block plants across Maharashtra, Karnataka, Uttar Pradesh, and other high-soiling regions.
highlights.h3: Integration paths for fixed-tilt (GLYDE) and tracker (GLYDE-X) layouts without sprinkler infrastructure.

automatic.gridIntro: Open a plant profile for MW capacity, geography, robot model, and operational context. Images and titles reflect the published case study; details vary by site.
automatic.closingHeading: Planning automatic robots on your plant?
automatic.closingBodyAfter: with DC capacity, row pitch, and tracker vs fixed-tilt layout for a site-specific proposal.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions, automatic projects
faq.subheading: What developers and O&M teams ask when reviewing Taypro automatic deployments.
faq.q0: Which plants are a good fit for automatic Taypro robots?
faq.a0: Ground-mount utility sites, often 50 MW and above, with repeatable row spacing, accessible blocks, and soiling or water constraints that make manual washing costly. Fixed-tilt arrays commonly use GLYDE; single-axis trackers use GLYDE-X with tracker-aware paths.
faq.q1: How is an automatic project different from semi-automatic or CAPEX?
faq.a1: Automatic listings feature fully autonomous fleets on fixed schedules. Semi-automatic listings highlight portable HELYX coverage for scattered blocks. CAPEX listings describe outright robot purchases; the same owner may also use Taypro Opex as a managed service, check the individual case study for the exact model.
faq.q2: What should I read before requesting a quote?
faq.a2: Pick the case study closest to your MW and region, read the automatic product page for technical limits, run the ROI calculator with your tariff and soiling assumptions, then share layout drawings with Taypro for cycle-frequency recommendations.

semiAutomatic.title: 半自動ソーラーパネル洗浄ロボット導入事例
semiAutomatic.description: 半自動ソーラーパネル洗浄ロボットの導入事例。ポータブルHELYXでブロック横断の柔軟な乾式洗浄。
semiAutomatic.introP1Before: Semi-automatic listings document plants where Taypro deploys
semiAutomatic.introP1After: across multiple blocks, uneven terrain, or phases where a full autonomous fleet is not yet justified. Crews move robots between rows while retaining waterless, brush-based cleaning quality.
semiAutomatic.introP2: These projects suit sites that need flexible coverage, staged commissioning, mixed topography, or supplemental cleaning alongside an existing automatic fleet. HELYX reduces dependence on manual labour and sprinklers while keeping capital exposure lower than a full GLYDE deployment.

highlights.h0: Portable robots and docking accessories moved between blocks as soiling patterns change.
highlights.h1: Dry cleaning without high-pressure water, important where water logistics are constrained.
highlights.h2: Use cases on multi-block plants, difficult access roads, and hybrid O&M strategies.
highlights.h3: A stepping stone toward full autonomy where layout or budget matures over time.

semiAutomatic.gridIntro: Each link opens a field note with plant scale, location, and how HELYX or related portable systems were used on site.
semiAutomatic.closingBodyAfter: with block layout, road access, and target cleaning frequency.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions, semi-automatic projects
faq.subheading: Common questions about HELYX-style portable deployments.
faq.q0: When should we choose semi-automatic instead of automatic robots?
faq.a0: When blocks are scattered, roads are difficult, capital is phased, or you need supplemental cleaning between automatic cycles. HELYX fits O&M teams that can assign crews to move robots rather than run a fixed autonomous fleet on every row.
faq.q1: Can semi-automatic robots coexist with GLYDE fleets?
faq.a1: Yes. Many owners deploy autonomous robots on the highest-soiling blocks and HELYX on peripheral or newly commissioned sections. Case studies note the mix when published.
faq.q2: How do I compare semi-automatic costs to CAPEX or Opex?
faq.a2: Review the HELYX product page, read a similar case study, then use the ROI calculator. Taypro can outline portable robot counts, crew time, and upgrade paths to GLYDE when layout allows.

capex.title: CAPEXソーラーパネル洗浄ロबोット導入事例
capex.description: CAPEX所有のソーラーパネル洗浄ロボット導入事例。社内O&M向けにTayproハードウェアを購入した顧客。
capex.introP1Before: CAPEX project listings show utilities and IPPs that purchased Taypro cleaning robots outright, capital on the balance sheet, operated by in-house or contracted O&M. This contrasts with
capex.introP1Link: Taypro Opex cleaning as a service
capex.introP1After: , where Taypro owns robots and runs cycles under SLA. Both models appear across India; the right choice depends on balance-sheet preference, internal O&M maturity, and payback horizon.
capex.introP2: CAPEX buyers typically want asset control, spare-parts stocking, and direct integration with plant SCADA and NECTYR. Taypro supports commissioning, operator training, and pan-India spare logistics after purchase. Documented CAPEX plants below show how owners sized fleets for MW, soiling class, and tracker vs fixed-tilt design.
capex.introP3After: , and read automatic or semi-automatic references for technology context.

highlights.h0: Outright purchase of GLYDE, GLYDE-X, or HELYX fleets with Taypro commissioning support.
highlights.h1: In-house O&M teams trained on NECTYR scheduling, rest zones, and robot health checks.
highlights.h2: Clear contrast with Opex, where Taypro retains ownership and performs cleaning under contract.
highlights.h3: Field evidence on payback drivers: soiling loss recovery, water savings, and labour redeployment.

capex.gridIntro: Select a plant to see how CAPEX procurement was structured and which robot models were deployed.
capex.closingHeading: Evaluating CAPEX vs Opex for your site?
capex.closingBodyAfter: about CAPEX fleet sizing, training, and spare support for your MW and layout.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions, CAPEX projects
faq.subheading: How asset owners compare buying robots vs Taypro-operated cleaning.
faq.q0: What does CAPEX mean in Taypro project listings?
faq.a0: The developer or IPP bought robots as capital equipment and operates them with internal or third-party O&M. Taypro delivers hardware, commissioning, and support; the owner carries depreciation and uptime responsibility.
faq.q1: How is CAPEX different from Taypro Opex?
faq.a1: Under Opex, Taypro owns the fleet and performs cleaning under SLA, useful when you want predictable opex line items and outsourced cycle execution. CAPEX suits teams with strong in-house O&M and appetite for asset ownership. The Opex service page explains service scope.
faq.q2: Can a CAPEX plant still use Taypro for operations?
faq.a2: Some owners buy hardware CAPEX and contract Taypro for monitoring or periodic audits. Case studies call out the model when published. Share your preferred split with Taypro during proposal.
